title: Linear Models, Regression, and Systems · Evidence Workshop
summary: Allocate staff and supplies under multiple linear constraints. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a linear allocation and scenario model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Fit and interpret linear rate and intercept parameters with units and domain limits.
Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.
Solve linear systems and classify unique, absent, and infinite solutions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A model passes through (3, 11) and (7, 23). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Linear Decision for a linear allocation and scenario model.
steps.why: The Linear Decision result for a linear allocation and scenario model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use sensitivity and shadow interpretations to defend a constrained linear decision.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Linear Decision, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a linear allocation and scenario model. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use sensitivity and shadow interpretations to defend a constrained linear decision.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.
